_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The smoking gun has been found.

The SSH key authenticates as `johnranel-cnx`, but the repository belongs to `imjohnranel`. This mismatch prevents pushing. The `[gone]` status on `origin/main` indicates the remote branch tracking is broken.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

You’re authenticated to GitHub as a **different account** than the one that owns the repo.

- **Repo you’re pushing to:** `imjohnranel/hirenerwin`
- **SSH identity Git is using:** `johnranel-cnx`

GitHub only allows pushes if that authenticated user has write access. `johnranel-cnx` doesn’t, so Cursor offers to **fork** instead.

Your local `user.name` / email (`imjohnranel` / `itsjohnranel@gmail.com`) don’t control push access — the **SSH key’s GitHub account** does.

### Fix options

1. **Push as `imjohnranel`**  
   Use an SSH key (or HTTPS token) tied to the `imjohnranel` account, then push again.

2. **Stay on `johnranel-cnx`**  
   Accept the fork, push there, and open a PR into `imjohnranel/hirenerwin` (if you have/get access), **or** get `johnranel-cnx` added as a collaborator on that repo.

3. **If this is your personal machine and you meant to use `imjohnranel`**  
   Check which key SSH is offering (`~/.ssh/config` / default key) and point `github.com` at the key for `imjohnranel`.

Also, `main...origin/main [gone]` means your local branch’s remote tracking ref is gone — often because the remote branch was deleted, the repo moved, or this account never had access.

**Bottom line:** Cursor isn’t wrong — your current GitHub login (`johnranel-cnx`) can’t write to `imjohnranel/hirenerwin`.

_**Agent (default)**_

`user.name` / `user.email` only set who appears on commits — they don’t change which GitHub account can push. Your SSH key is still authenticating as `johnranel-cnx`. Checking which key GitHub is using so we can point it at `imjohnranel`.

Multiple GitHub accounts are configured with Host aliases. The remote points to the default github.com host instead of github-personal. The SSH agent offers the first matching key, which belongs to the loveme account. This causes the redirect to imjohnranel.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

_**Agent (default)**_

Those settings only change the **name on commits**. They do **not** choose which GitHub account logs in.

Push auth is still SSH. Right now:

1. Your remote is `git@github.com:imjohnranel/hirenerwin.git` (plain `github.com`)
2. Your `~/.ssh/config` maps **`imjohnranel` to `Host github-personal`**, not to `github.com`
3. Connecting to `github.com` uses whatever key the agent offers first — and GitHub greets you as **`johnranel-cnx`**

So git thinks the author is `imjohnranel`, but GitHub still sees the push as `johnranel-cnx`.

### Fix for this repo

Point `origin` at your personal host alias:

```powershell
git remote set-url origin git@github-personal:imjohnranel/hirenerwin.git
ssh -T git@github-personal
git push -u origin main
```

`ssh -T` should say `Hi imjohnranel!` before you push.

### Why this happened

You already set up account aliases (`github-personal`, `github-loveme`, etc.), but this repo’s remote still uses `github.com`, so it never picks the personal key.
